chunk_summary: Focuses on infection prevention in feeding under-twos through hand hygiene, safe water, clean feeding practices, and food storage to reduce contamination and growth faltering risks.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of handwashing with soap and safe water in infant feeding routines.
Learn safe water practices, including boiling and proper storage, to prevent recontamination.
Identify hygienic feeding equipment and food preparation methods to minimize bacterial risks.
Recognize signs of recurrent infection and integrate hygiene into clinical follow-up for growth monitoring.
key_facts: Handwashing with soap and safe water is essential before food preparation, feeding, and after diaper changes.
Boil water for drinking and food preparation in households with uncertain water safety; store in clean, covered containers with narrow openings.
Cooked foods left at room temperature in hot climates should be refrigerated within 2 hours or reheated until steaming hot before the next feed.
Avoid routine bottle use in low-sanitation settings due to cleaning challenges; emphasize immediate washing and drying if bottles are used.
Recurrent diarrhea, oral thrush, or poor appetite may indicate hygiene issues affecting growth and require clinical review.
reference_quote: Teach handwashing with soap and safe water before preparing food, before feeding, and after diaper changes; as self-feeding begins, wash the child’s hands as well. (WHO guideline complementary feeding)
document_key_topics: Clean feeding equipment and food storage
